reviews.review_msg: Loves his job, pretty fun and lectures are super helpful. Lots of material medium difficulty so takes a good amount of work, exams are fair but hard. Decent amount of assignments etc to get your grade up a bit. (BUS 251)
One of the best Financial Accojntjng teachers you can get. GOAT. All I can say.
Not an easy course but way more tolerable than I expected. Richard was a big reason for that. Hope he teaches more courses at SFU!

Great tax prof - I had heard terrible things about tax prior to taking it. Tax is tough - but Richard made it more bearable. Be careful, there are 2 Richard Wong's that BUS courses @ SFU. One teaches accounting, the other teaches computing systems. I almost unenrolled b/c of the ratings on RateMyProfs. But you'll want to go look him up at UBC.

Chris was an amazing professor, especially during his first semester ever of teaching. There are tons of concepts to remember, and it can be overwhelming, but use the textbook as reference and KEEP UP with the practice questions (do it over and over to study). Exams are lengthy and mostly calculations, but Chris lets you know exactly what is on it.

I thought she was a great prof. Her notes were detailed and enough to get by in her class. I used the textbook to get more insight into the more complex chapters. She would tell you what was important and what was not required to know. The work in 330 is not complicated but does require a lot of memorization for tax deductions and credits.
Really great prof, you can tell he cares about his students, has very fair exams, and will go out of his way to help you if you ask! the slides examples are comprehensive and well explained as well!

Weiming is great. He structures the course and exams very fairly, makes sure to emphasize exam topics in class, and is extremely approachable. One of the more genuine and kindhearted professors I've encountered at SFU. I definitely recommend.

I bombed Exam 1, receiving the lowest grade due to my incompetence. But Weiming's encouragement in Exam 2 helped me perform years better, getting near the highest mark! He adjusted the exam weight if we do better in Exam 2. I was considering dropping the course, and glad I didn't. His exams are fair, and he's like a nice dad + allows a cheat sheet!
Not bad overall, the exams were easy compare to the lecture.
he is hard, doesn't explain material very well, always skips the main points in the textbook.kk

This professor is consistently rude and dismissive. Questions are met with sarcasm or eye-rolling, and it feels like you're being talked down to rather than taught. Office hours are uncomfortable and unhelpful, as they seem irritated that students even show up. The material could be interesting, but the hostile attitude makes it hard.

Would not recommend. Lectures are all over the place and rarely match the exams. The professor reads straight off the slides and doesn't explain things clearly. Asking questions feels pointless because answers are vague or dismissive. Grading is harsh and inconsistent, with very little feedback.
His teaching style is very different and difficult to understand. The exams are not what he teaches. He does however have knowledge about the topic but his accent makes it hard.
beware of the positive reviews they are setting up a trap

Engaging lectures.
Homework marked based on effort combination of Canvas and Pearson assignments.

Tests are fair - he tells you what's on it lol

GO TO LECTURE; if you don't, do be surprised you fail lol.

No pictures in his class so your notes have to be written/typed.

He is an excellent educator, and highly qualified individual. He incorporates stories from his actual experiences and what you learn is directly applicable to real life. Was the class difficult? Yes, but tax is difficult and so is the CFE. Growth requires you to be uncomfortable sometimes... and I'm okay with that.
Choose another instructor rather than her please!

She is the best professor I have at SFU. She is always friendly and clearly communicates what is important and what will be tested. For those who complained, it seems they aren't putting in the extra effort to attend office hours or stay after class for help.

A little too much reading off of the slides. I would recommend printing the slides and noting things she says that aren't already on the slides. Besides that, pretty average, but tax is a difficult topic, so maybe that means she's above average.

Teresa is a good professor. She explains the tax rules and concepts very clearly and states what will and will not be on the exams. During online instruction, she was very considerate to students and made lectures asynchronous while the scheduled class time was used as an (optional to attend) live review and Q&A session. Very approachable.
